phrase.ph: 【悪：わる】【口：ぐち】を【浴：あ】びせる
phrase.tr_en: to hurl abuse at so.; to shower so. with insults
phrase.rem: jisho has n5 for abiru, nx for abiseru

sentence.s: 【人：ひと】の【陰：かげ】で【悪：わる】【口：ぐち】を【言：い】うな。
sentence.rom: Hito no kage de waruguchi o iu na.
sentence.tr_en: Don't speak ill of others behind their back.
sentence.tr_de: Rede nicht schlecht über andere hinter ihrem Rücken.

sentence.s: 【彼：かの】【女：じょ】は【私：わたし】に【悪：わる】【口：ぐち】を【浴：あ】びせた。
sentence.rom: Kanojo wa watashi ni waruguchi o abiseta.
sentence.tr_en: She hurled abuse at me.
sentence.tr_de: Sie überschüttete mich mit Beleidungen.
sentence.rem: verified with ChatGPT; jisho has n5 for abiru, nx for abiseru

sentence.s: 【一：いち】【番：ばん】【近：ちか】い【薬：やっ】【局：きょく】はどこですか。
sentence.rom: Ichiban chikai yakkyoku wa doko desu ka?
sentence.tr_en: Where is the nearest drugstore?
sentence.tr_de: Wo ist die nächste Apotheke?

word.rem: is now a phrase of kyoku; jisho has n2
appears in GENKI2 L23 vocab section; ChatGPT disagrees jisho and says it isn't a suru verb

phrase.ph: いい【結：けっ】【果：か】を【得：え】る

phrase.tr_en: to be surprised by the results

sentence.s: チーム【全：ぜん】【員：いん】の【協：きょう】【力：りょく】で、いい【結：けっ】【果：か】を【得：え】ることができた。
sentence.rom: Chīmu zen'in no kyōryoku de, ii kekka o eru koto ga dekita.
sentence.tr_en: Because of the team's cooperation, we were able to achieve a good result.
sentence.tr_de: Aufgrund der Zusammenarbeit im Team konnten wir ein gutes Resultat erzielen.

phrase.ph: 【集：しゅう】【中：ちゅう】しやすい【書：しょ】【斎：さい】
phrase.tr_en: a study room that is easy to concentrate in
phrase.tr_de: ein Studierzimmer, in welchem man sich gut konzentrieren kann

sentence.s: 【眠：ねむ】くて、【集：しゅう】【中：ちゅう】できない。
sentence.rom: Nemukute, shūchū dekinai.
sentence.tr_en: I'm sleepy and can't concentrate.
sentence.tr_de: Ich bin schläfrig und kann mich nicht konzentrieren.

sentence.s: この【部：へ】【屋：や】は【煩：うるさ】くて、【集：しゅう】【中：ちゅう】しにくい。
sentence.rom: Kono heya wa urusakute, shūchū shi-nikui.
sentence.tr_en: This room is noisy, and it's difficult to concentrate.
sentence.tr_de: Dieser Raum ist laut, es ist schwer, sich zu konzentrieren.

phrase.ph: きれいな【女：じょ】【優：ゆう】

sentence.s: デパートで【有：ゆう】【名：めい】な【女：じょ】【優：ゆう】を【見：み】ました。
sentence.rom: Depāto de yūmei na joyū o mimashita.
sentence.tr_en: I saw a famous actress at a department store.
sentence.tr_de: Ich sah eine berühmte Schauspielerin in einem Kaufhaus.

sentence.s: このパンは【小：こ】【麦：むぎ】から【作：つく】られています。
sentence.rom: Kono pan wa komugi kara tsukurarete imasu.
sentence.tr_en: This bread is made from wheat.
sentence.tr_de: Dieses Brot wird aus Weizen gemacht.

word.rem: jisho has nx; just to provide the reading for the furigana check of sentences

phrase.rem: from jisho; verified with Google; Wikipedia also has 電気機関車
verified with ChatGPT; jisho has n2
verified with jisho; jisho has nx

sentence.s: 【機：き】【関：かん】【車：しゃ】は【客：きゃく】【車：しゃ】を【引：ひ】く。
sentence.rom: Kikansha wa kyakusha o hiku.
sentence.tr_en: The locomotive pulls the passenger cars.
sentence.tr_de: Die Lokomotive zieht die Passagierwagons.
sentence.rem: reworded by ChatGPT; jisho has nx for kyakusha, but n4 for kyaku, n5 for densha

word.rem: is now a phrase of kikan
is now a phrase of kikan
appears in GENKI2 L21 vocab section

sentence.s: 【文：もん】【句：く】を【言：い】えば【切：き】りがない。
sentence.rom: Monku o ieba kiri ga nai.
sentence.tr_en: Once the complaining starts, it never ends.
sentence.tr_de: Wenn die Beschwerden einmal anfangen, hören sie nicht mehr auf.
sentence.explanation_en: Or, depending on context: If I complained, there would be no end.
sentence.explanation_de: Oder, abhängig vom Kontext: Wenn ich mich beklagen würde, könnte ich nicht mehr aufhören.

sentence.s: 【文：もん】【句：く】を【言：い】っても【始：はじ】まらないだろ。
sentence.rom: Monku o itte mo hajimaranai daro.
sentence.tr_en: Complaining won't change anything.
sentence.tr_de: Sich beschweren bringt gar nichts.
sentence.explanation_en: 「始まらない」can have the meaning of 'there's no point'
sentence.explanation_de: 「始まらない」kann die Bedeutung von 'es bringt nichts' haben
sentence.rem: 始まらない verified with jisho; jisho has nx for this meaning

sentence.s: 【歩：ほ】【道：どう】を【歩：ある】けば【安：あん】【全：ぜん】だよ。
sentence.rom: Hodō o arukeba anzen da yo.
sentence.tr_en: You'll be safer walking on the pavement.
sentence.tr_de: Auf dem Bürgersteig gehen wäre sicherer.

phrase.ph: 【偉：い】【大：だい】な【発：はつ】【明：めい】

sentence.s: 【研：けん】【究：きゅう】【者：しゃ】は【新：あたら】しい【技：ぎ】【術：じゅつ】を【発：はつ】【明：めい】しました。
sentence.rom: Kenkyūsha wa atarashii gijutsu o hatsumei shimashita.
sentence.tr_en: The researcher invented a new technique.
sentence.tr_de: Der Forscher erfand eine neue Technik.

phrase.tr_en: at the bottom of the sea
at the bottom of a box
hearty; from the bottom of my heart
phrase.tr_de: von Herzen; vom Grund meines Herzens

sentence.s: 【瓶：びん】の【底：そこ】に【何：なに】かが【沈：しず】んでいる。
sentence.rom: Bin no soko ni nanika ga shizunde iru.
sentence.tr_en: Something has settled at the bottom of the bottle.
sentence.tr_de: Etwas hat sich am Boden der Flasche gesetzt.
sentence.rem: ChatGPT says ている here means a resulting state, not an ongoing action

sentence.s: お【金：かね】が【底：そこ】をついた。
sentence.rom: O-kane ga soko o tsuita.
sentence.tr_en: I ran out of money.
sentence.tr_de: Mein Geld ist aufgebraucht.

word.tr_en: staying up all night; to stay up all night
word.tr_de: Wachbleiben die ganze Nacht; die Nacht durchzechen
word.rem: appears in GENKI2 L22 vocab section

phrase.tr_en: to frequently stay up all night
phrase.tr_de: häufig die ganze Nacht wach bleiben

sentence.s: 【徹：てつ】【夜：や】で【勉：べん】【強：きょう】した。
sentence.rom: Tetsuya de benkyō shita.
sentence.tr_en: I studied all night.
sentence.tr_de: Ich studierte die ganze Nacht durch.

sentence.s: 【小：しょう】【説：せつ】を【読：よ】んで【徹：てつ】【夜：や】した。
sentence.rom: Shōsetsu o yonde tetsuya shita.
sentence.tr_en: I stayed up all night reading a novel.
sentence.tr_de: Ich bin die ganze Nacht wachgeblieben und habe einen Roman gelesen.

word.rem: appears in 500mon q292; appears in GENKI2 L21 vocab section; jisho has nx

phrase.ph: 【授：じゅ】【業：ぎょう】【料：りょう】を【払：はら】う

sentence.s: 【授：じゅ】【業：ぎょう】【料：りょう】、【今日：きょう】【払：はら】わなくてもいいですが、【明日：あした】、【必：かなら】ずお【願：ねが】いします。
sentence.rom: Jugyōryō, kyō harawanakute mo ii desu ga, ashita, kanarazu o-negai shimasu.
sentence.tr_en: You don't have to pay tuition today, but please be sure to do so tomorrow.
sentence.tr_de: Sie müssen die Studiengebühr nicht heute zahlen, aber morgen bitte unbedingt.

word.rem: appears in GENKI2 L22 vocab section

sentence.s: 【話：はな】し【相：あい】【手：て】がほしい。
sentence.rom: Hanashi-aite ga hoshii.
sentence.tr_en: I need someone to talk with.
sentence.tr_de: Ich brauche jemanden, mit dem ich reden kann.

sentence.s: 【僕：ぼく】には【相：そう】【談：だん】【相：あい】【手：て】がない。
sentence.rom: Boku ni wa sōdan-aite ga nai.
sentence.tr_en: I have no one to turn to for advice.
sentence.tr_de: Ich habe niemanden, den ich um Rat fragen könnte.

sentence.s: 【工：こう】【場：じょう】を【建：た】てる【計：けい】【画：かく】が【中：ちゅう】【止：し】になった。
sentence.rom: Kōjō o tateru keikaku ga chūshi ni natta.
sentence.tr_en: The plan to build a factory has been abandoned.
sentence.tr_de: Der Plan, eine Fabrik zu bauen, wurde aufgegeben.
sentence.rem: jisho has n3 for keikaku, chūshi

phrase.ph: ロシア【大：だい】【統：とう】【領：りょう】

sentence.s: 【新：あたら】しい【大：だい】【統：とう】【領：りょう】は【評：ひょう】【判：ばん】がよい。
sentence.rom: Atarashii daitōryō wa hyōban ga yoi.
sentence.tr_en: The new president has a good reputation.
sentence.tr_de: Der neue Präsident hat einen guten Ruf.

sentence.s: 【座：ざ】【席：せき】には【番：ばん】【号：ごう】が【付：つ】いていますか。
sentence.rom: Zaseki ni wa bangō ga tsuite imasu ka?
sentence.tr_en: Are the seats numbered?
sentence.tr_de: Sind die Plätze nummeriert?

sentence.s: スポーツ【大：たい】【会：かい】に【参：さん】【加：か】することは【健：けん】【康：こう】にいいです。
sentence.rom: Supōtsu taikai ni sanka suru koto wa kenkō ni ii desu.
sentence.tr_en: Participating in a sports event is good for your health.
sentence.tr_de: An einem Sportturnier teilnehmen ist gut für die Gesundheit.

sentence.s: 【数：すう】【学：がく】は【難：むずか】しい【科：か】【目：もく】だ。
sentence.rom: Sūgaku wa muzukashii kamoku da.
sentence.tr_en: Mathematics is a difficult subject.
sentence.tr_de: Mathematik ist ein schwieriges Fach.

phrase.ph: 【銀：ぎん】【行：こう】【強：ごう】【盗：とう】をする
phrase.rem: also possible: 銀行を強盗する, but ChatGPT says the other one is more natural; DeepL has both

sentence.s: 【警：けい】【察：さつ】は【強：ごう】【盗：とう】を【逮：たい】【捕：ほ】した。
sentence.rom: Keisatsu wa gōtō o taiho shita.
sentence.tr_en: The police arrested the burglar.
sentence.tr_de: Die Polizei hat den Einbrecher verhaftet.

word.rem: appears in GENKI2 L22 vocab section

phrase.ph: 【計：けい】【画：かく】に【賛：さん】【成：せい】する

sentence.s: 【私：わたし】は【賛：さん】【成：せい】です。
sentence.rom: Watashi wa sansei desu.
sentence.tr_en: I agree.
sentence.tr_de: Ich stimme zu.

sentence.s: 【私：わたし】はその【意：い】【見：けん】に【賛：さん】【成：せい】じゃないよ。
sentence.rom: Watashi wa sono iken ni sansei janai yo.
sentence.tr_en: I do not agree with that opinion.
sentence.tr_de: Ich bin nicht dieser Meinung.

sentence.s: それを【郵：ゆう】【便：びん】で【送：おく】ってくれ。
sentence.rom: Sore o yūbin de okutte kure.
sentence.tr_en: Send it to me by mail.
sentence.tr_de: Schicke es mir per Post.

sentence.s: この【辺：あた】りは【夕：ゆう】【方：がた】になると【静：しず】かになります。
sentence.rom: Kono atari wa yūgata ni naru to shizuka ni narimasu.
sentence.tr_en: This area of town becomes quiet in the evening.
sentence.tr_de: In dieser Gegend wird es abends still.

sentence.s: 【美：び】【術：じゅつ】【館：かん】で【絵：かい】【画：が】を【見：み】るのが【好：す】きです。
sentence.rom: Bijutsukan de kaiga o miru no ga suki desu.
sentence.tr_en: I like to look at paintings in the art museum.
sentence.tr_de: Ich mag es, Gemälde im Kunstmuseum anzuschauen.

phrase.ph: 【化：ばけ】【学：がく】の【実：じっ】【験：けん】
【行：こう】【動：どう】を【説：せつ】【明：めい】する
【急：きゅう】【速：そく】に【変：へん】【化：か】する【社：しゃ】【会：かい】

word.tr_en: stalk (of a mushroom, leaf); handle (of a tool)

word.rem: appears in GENKI2 p.94 vocab section as suisenjō

phrase.rem: appears in vocab section; jisho has nx

sentence.s: 【山：やま】【口：ぐち】さんを【大：だい】【学：がく】【教：きょう】【授：じゅ】に【推：すい】【薦：せん】します。
sentence.rom: Yamaguchi-san o daigaku-kyōju ni suisen shimasu.
sentence.tr_en: I recommend Mr Yamaguchi for (the position of) university professor.
sentence.tr_de: Ich empfehle Herrn Yamaguchi für (die Position als) Universitätsprofessor.

phrase.tr_en: waste of money; waste of time
phrase.rem: appears in GENKI2 L22 vocab section; jisho has n1

word.rem: appears in GENKI2 p.286 kanji section as 水不足 mizubusoku

sentence.s: その【靴：くつ】は【本：ほん】【革：がわ】で【作：つく】られていますか。
sentence.rom: Sono kutsu wa hongawa de tsukurarete imasu ka?
sentence.tr_en: Are those shoes made of real leather?
sentence.tr_de: Sind diese Schuhe aus echtem Leder?
sentence.rem: verified with ChatGPT; jisho has nx for hongawa; jisho says hon-kawa is also correct

sentence.s: その【革：かわ】は【本：ほん】【物：もの】ではありません。
sentence.rom: Sono kawa wa honmono de wa arimasen.
sentence.tr_en: That leather is not genuine.
sentence.tr_de: Dieses Leder ist nicht echt.

phrase.ph: 【列：れつ】を【作：つく】って【並：なら】ぶ
phrase.tr_en: to line up in a queue

word.hint_en: typical age range from 18 to 30; formal

phrase.rem: jisho has nx for dan, but n3 for dantai

sentence.s: 【息：むす】【子：こ】さんも【青：せい】【年：ねん】になったね。
sentence.rom: Musuko-san mo seinen ni natta ne.
sentence.tr_en: Your son has become a young man, hasn't he?
sentence.tr_de: Dein Sohn ist ein Erwachsener geworden, nicht wahr?

phrase.ph: ご【参：さん】【加：か】の【方：かた】【々：がた】

sentence.s: 【王：おう】【様：さま】は【亡：な】くなった【方：かた】【々：がた】のためにお【祈：いの】りをなさいました。
sentence.rom: Ō-sama wa nakunatta kata-gata no tame ni o-inori o nasaimashita.
sentence.tr_en: The King offered prayers for those who had passed away.
sentence.tr_de: Der König sprach Gebete für die Verstorbenen.

sentence.s: その【仕：し】【事：ごと】、やっぱり【面：めん】【倒：どう】になっちゃった。
sentence.rom: Sono shigoto, yappari mendō ni nacchatta.
sentence.tr_en: That job has become a hassle after all.
sentence.tr_de: Diese Arbeit ist letztendlich doch mühsam geworden.

phrase.ph: 【突：とつ】【然：ぜん】の【出：で】【来：き】【事：ごと】に【驚：おどろ】く
phrase.tr_en: to be surprised by a sudden event

sentence.s: 【本：ほん】【当：とう】にあった【出：で】【来：き】【事：ごと】だよ。
sentence.rom: Hontō ni atta dekigoto da yo.
sentence.tr_en: It really happened.
sentence.tr_de: Es ist wirklich passiert.

phrase.rem: verified with ChatGPT; jisho has n1 for kiri, but n5 for kiru

word.rem: appears in GENKI2 p.329 kanji section

sentence.s: 【私：わたし】は【友：とも】【達：だち】がいないことは【不：ふ】【幸：こう】だと【思：おも】う。
sentence.rom: Watashi wa tomodachi ga inai koto wa fukō da to omou.
sentence.tr_en: I think that having no friends is a tragedy.
sentence.tr_de: Ich finde, keine Freunde haben ist eine Tragödie.
